title: Spain World Cup 2026 Roster: De la Fuente's Squad and Lamine Yamal Status
original_text:
Spain's World Cup 2026 squad: de la Fuente's 26 and the bid for a second star
Luis de la Fuente named Spain's 26-man World Cup 2026 squad on May 25, 2026, captained by Rodri and built without a single Real Madrid player for the first time since 1950. Teenage star Lamine Yamal is included but is a doubt for the June 15 opener against Cape Verde as he recovers from a hamstring injury. Below are all 26 players by position, Spain's Group H fixtures, the Yamal update, and where to back La Roja among our World Cup 2026 betting sites.
Last updated: June 2, 2026. This is Spain's final 26, confirmed ahead of the June 1 FIFA deadline. Tournament rules allow an injured player to be replaced up to 24 hours before the opener, so the list reflects the squad as confirmed on announcement night.
(Photo by Alex Caparros/Getty Images)
Spain World Cup 2026 roster TL;DR
Head coach: Luis de la Fuente (Euro 2024 winner, in charge since 2022)
Roster status: Final 26-man squad, announced May 25, 2026
Captain: Rodri (Manchester City)
Key player: Lamine Yamal (Barcelona), doubtful for the opener (hamstring)
Group: Group H, with Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia and Uruguay
First match: June 15 vs Cape Verde, Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta (12:00 PM ET)
Squad makeup: 3 goalkeepers, 8 defenders, 7 midfielders, 8 forwards
World Cup history: Champions in 2010, European champions (Euro 2024), 17th World Cup appearance
Spain World Cup 2026 roster: full squad list
(Photo by Juan Manuel Serrano Arce/Getty Images)
De la Fuente leaned heavily on La Liga and on a Barcelona core, naming eight Barcelona players and, in a first since 1950, none from Real Madrid. The list groups every player by position, with the club each was attached to at selection. Lamine Yamal is the name carrying a fitness question into the opener.
PositionPlayerClubNote
Goalkeeper Unai Simón Athletic Club
Goalkeeper David Raya Arsenal
Goalkeeper Joan García Barcelona
Defender Marc Cucurella Chelsea
Defender Alejandro Grimaldo Bayer Leverkusen
Defender Pau Cubarsí Barcelona

Defender Pedro Porro Tottenham Hotspur

Midfielder Pedri Barcelona

Midfielder Martín Zubimendi Arsenal
Midfielder Gavi Barcelona
Midfielder Álex Baena Atlético Madrid
Midfielder Mikel Merino Arsenal
Forward Lamine Yamal Barcelona Doubtful for the June 15 opener (hamstring)
Forward Nico Williams Athletic Club Fitness doubt
Forward Mikel Oyarzabal Real Sociedad
Forward Dani Olmo Barcelona
Forward Ferran Torres Barcelona
Forward Yéremy Pino Crystal Palace
Forward Borja Iglesias Celta Vigo
Forward Víctor Muñoz Osasuna
Squad total: 26 (3 goalkeepers, 8 defenders, 7 midfielders, 8 forwards).
Is Lamine Yamal in Spain's World Cup 2026 squad?
Yes. Lamine Yamal is in the squad for his first World Cup, but he is a doubt for the June 15 opener against Cape Verde as he recovers from a hamstring tear that has kept him out since late April. De la Fuente said he expects Yamal and Spain's other injured players to be ready for the first or second group game, while some Spanish reports suggest his return may come later in the group stage.
(Photo by Stu Forster/Getty Images)
Yamal is the player the tournament will watch. He announced himself at Euro 2024 with a stunning semi-final goal against France on the way to the title, and at 18 he is already one of the best wingers in the world. Spain will manage his return carefully rather than rush him back for a game against Cape Verde they expect to win.
He is not the only fitness question. Fellow winger Nico Williams has had an injury-disrupted season and is also a doubt for the opener, so Spain could begin the tournament without both first-choice wide men. Captain Rodri, the 2024 Ballon d'Or winner, anchors the midfield after returning from the knee injury that cost him most of last season, with Pedri alongside him. This section will be updated as Yamal's status develops.
Who was left out of Spain's World Cup 2026 roster?
The story of the squad is who is not in it. For the first time since 1950, de la Fuente named no Real Madrid players at all. Defender Dean Huijsen was ruled out by injury, and veteran right-back Dani Carvajal, who is leaving Madrid this summer, was overlooked after an injury-hit campaign. Madrid's other Spanish options, among them Raúl Asencio and Fran García, did not make the cut.
Beyond the Madrid contingent, Atlético Madrid's Robin Le Normand missed out, and Barcelona's Fermín López was ruled out by a broken foot. Álvaro Morata, Spain's captain at Euro 2024, was also left out as de la Fuente leaned into the next generation.
Spain World Cup 2026 fixtures
(Photo by Kevin C. Cox/Getty Images)
Spain play their first two group games in Atlanta before crossing into Mexico for the finale. They do not play in Canada during the group stage.
DateOpponentVenueTime (ET)
Mon, June 15 Cape Verde Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta 12:00 PM
Sun, June 21 Saudi Arabia Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta 12:00 PM
Fri, June 26 Uruguay Estadio Akron, Guadalajara (Mexico) 8:00 PM
All times Eastern. For the latest match prices as the group unfolds, our soccer odds page tracks Spain's lines.
Spain World Cup 2026 odds comparison
Spain go into the World Cup as one of the favourites to win it all, priced just ahead of or level with France at the very top of the board. Most books price La Roja between 4.80 and 5.75 in decimal, ahead of England, Brazil and Argentina. The table below compares each sportsbook's price for Spain to lift the trophy. Outright odds move with form, injuries and results, so confirm the live price before betting, and see our World Cup 2026 betting sites guide for the full tournament market.

Stake is the standout at 5.75, with a cluster of 5.60 at 1xbet, BetLabel, 22bet and 10bet just behind. Most other books sit at 5.50 to 5.53, while BetPlays and 29Black are the short outliers at 4.80. “Odds were captured simultaneously on June 2, 2026 at 8:00 AM ET and move constantly. Confirm the live price at the sportsbook before placing any bet. 19+ (18+ in Alberta, Manitoba and Quebec). Please play responsibly. ”
Sol Fayerman-HansenEditor-in-Chief
Editor's take: Spain's World Cup 2026 chances
Spain play the best football at this World Cup, and on talent alone they are level with France at the top of my list. De la Fuente kept the Euro 2024 core and trusts it completely, leaving out every Real Madrid player without blinking. When Pedri and Rodri control midfield and Yamal and Nico Williams stretch the game, no defence in the world is comfortable. Group H is kind, with Uruguay the one side likely to make them work.
“The cloud is fitness, and it is a real one. Yamal's hamstring and Nico Williams' fitness mean Spain may open without both first-choice wingers, and how quickly they get to full strength matters. If the key men are sharp by the knockout rounds, I have Spain winning it. If the injuries linger, the gap to the chasing pack narrows fast. For now they stay among my top two. ”
Sol Fayerman-HansenEditor-in-Chief
Roster status and changes
This is Spain's final 26. Under tournament rules, a player ruled out by a serious injury can be replaced up to 24 hours before the opener, so a late change remains possible if a fitness issue worsens.
Fitness is the main storyline. De la Fuente said he had no doubt that Lamine Yamal, Nico Williams and Mikel Merino would be ready, though Yamal and Williams are realistic doubts for the opener. Rodri returns as captain after recovering from the knee injury that wrecked his last club season. Spain play warm-up friendlies against Iraq in A Coruña and Peru in Puebla, Mexico before the tournament, the final tune-ups before Atlanta. Any official change will be reflected here, in the player table and in the FAQ.

FAQs on Team Spain at World Cup 2026
Common Questions on Spain World Cup 2026
Luis de la Fuente named 26 players: three goalkeepers, eight defenders, seven midfielders and eight forwards. The headline names are captain Rodri (Manchester City), Lamine Yamal (Barcelona), Pedri (Barcelona) and Nico Williams (Athletic Club). Eight of the 26 are Barcelona players, and none are from Real Madrid. The full list by position is in the table above.
Lamine Yamal is in the squad but is a doubt for the June 15 opener against Cape Verde as he recovers from a hamstring tear that has kept him out since late April. De la Fuente expects him ready for the first or second group game, while some Spanish reports suggest his return may come later. He is 18 and playing in his first World Cup.
Luis de la Fuente, who led Spain to the Euro 2024 title and has managed the team since 2022, when he replaced Luis Enrique. He named the 26-man squad on May 25, 2026, and Spain enter the tournament as European champions chasing a second World Cup to add to their 2010 win.
Spain announced the final 26-man squad on Monday, May 25, 2026, at an event in Madrid. June 1 was the FIFA deadline for final squads, so the roster is official, subject only to the standard injury-replacement window before the first match.
Spain is in Group H with Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia and Uruguay. The first two games are in Atlanta and the third is in Guadalajara, Mexico, so Spain do not play in Canada during the group stage. The top two teams in each group advance to the round of 32, along with the eight best third-placed teams across the 48-nation tournament.
Spain opens against Cape Verde on Monday, June 15, 2026 at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta, kickoff 12:00 PM ET. They then face Saudi Arabia on June 21 in Atlanta and Uruguay on June 26 in Guadalajara, Mexico. Spain are the 2010 world champions and reigning European champions.
It is the first Spain World Cup squad without a Real Madrid player since 1950. De la Fuente said he does not pick on the basis of club. Madrid's strongest candidate, Dean Huijsen, was ruled out by injury, Dani Carvajal was overlooked after an injury-hit season, and the coach preferred other options elsewhere. He insisted the decisions were sporting ones.
